articles.full_text:
Kerala heat warning: High temperatures likely to continue till Sunday, yellow alert issued in 12 districts
The IMD has issued a yellow alert for high temperatures across 12 Kerala districts until Sunday, with risks of heatstroke, sunburn, and dehydration due to a high UV index. Authorities have advised avoiding direct sun exposure (11 am-3 pm), staying hydrated, and wearing light clothing. Special caution has been urged for vulnerable groups and outdoor workers amidst the rising heat.
New Delhi: Sounding a warning, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has said that high temperatures are expected to continue across Kerala till Sunday. It has also issued a yellow alert in 12 districts. Only Idukki and Wayanad districts are not under the alert issued by IMD.
A high ultraviolet (UV) index, along with rising heat and humidity, may spike the risk of heatstroke, sunburn and dehydration, the State Disaster Management Authority cautioned. Temperatures are likely to go up to around 40°C in Palakkad, 39°C in Kollam, and 38°C in Kottayam, as per officials.
‘Avoid exposure to direct sunlight’
